name: Jurisdiction-Gated Diversity Factor Lookup
formula_reference: BS 7671:2018+A3 Appendix 1; IEC 60364-1 Annex C; NEC Article 220.61, 220.84 (multi-dwelling), 220.86 (commercial); CIBSE Guide K; ASHRAE Standard 90.1
implementation_note: Applies jurisdiction-appropriate diversity factor table to a mixed load list. BS 7671 Appendix 1 gives per-load-type rows (lighting 66%, sockets 40%, cooker first 10A + 30% of remainder, etc.). NEC 220.61 uses a different approach (general-purpose lighting at 100% of first 3 kVA, 35% of next 117 kVA, 25% beyond). IEC 60364-1 Annex C gives national-practice ranges. Inline LLM math reliably mis-applies the jurisdiction's specific banding (especially the NEC step-rate). Multi-table lookup + jurisdiction switching → tool-executed. Output drives db-layout busbar sizing and load-schedule mains sizing.

inputs.description: Standards family that drives the diversity table
Building / occupancy class — picks the correct table row

JSON array of loads: [{type: 'lighting'|'sockets'|'cooker'|'water_heater'|'motor'|'hvac'|'fixed_appliance', load_kw: number, count: int}]. Each entry processed against jurisdiction-appropriate diversity factor.
Headroom % added to the diversified total (industry standard 20-30%)

diversified_load_kw.description: Total load after diversity applied per load type

diversified_load_a.description: Diversified current at the declared supply voltage + phase arrangement

future_expansion_kw.description: Headroom added beyond diversified — separate line item

recommended_main_a.description: Next standard main switch / busbar rating ≥ diversified + future expansion

table_reference.description: Standard + clause cited (e.g. 'BS 7671 Appendix 1 Table A1', 'NEC 220.61')

runtime_contract.response: { ok: true, result: { diversified_load_kw, diversified_load_a, diversity_factor_overall, breakdown_by_type, future_expansion_kw, recommended_main_a, table_reference }, error?: string }
